input.description: Implement normalizer managed AES result model
input.prompt:
1. TASK: Implement the first production slice for the AES comparison CLI: namespace setup, plaintext normalization, managed AES encryptor, and CLI result model/formatter.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: The app project in D:\aesdemo\src\AesComparison.Cli gains the production types required by these tests: PlaintextNormalizerTests.cs, ManagedAesEncryptorTests.cs, and CliOutputModelTests.cs. Those tests should pass after your changes. AesNiEncryptorTests may still fail or be pending.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: read, apply_patch, bash, lsp_diagnostics. Use bash only for dotnet test/build verification.
4. MUST DO: Create clean production files under the app project with namespace AesComparison.Cli. Implement PlaintextNormalizer.Normalize(string) using UTF-8 and zero-padding to exactly 16 bytes, throwing ArgumentException for inputs whose UTF-8 length exceeds 16. Implement ManagedAesEncryptor with 16-byte key validation and AES-128/ECB/NoPadding single-block encryption using System.Security.Cryptography.Aes. Implement EncryptionResult and ResultFormatter so the current tests compile and pass, including ToConsoleOutput(). Keep behavior aligned to the tests exactly. Preserve net10.0 project setup. Run focused tests for the three targeted test files and fix issues until green.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not implement AesNiEncryptor yet. Do not add third-party packages. Do not weaken tests. Do not touch the AES-NI test file unless a compile-only adjustment is unavoidable through production surface compatibility.
6. CONTEXT: Workspace is an empty project scaffold plus tests. The fixed demo baseline is AES-128/ECB/NoPadding, single 16-byte block, fixed 16-byte key, UTF-8 plaintext normalized to one block. The tests currently fail because production types do not exist. You should make only the minimum production changes for this slice.

input.prompt: Continue from where you left off. If implementation is already complete, finish verification and summarize exact files changed plus current test status. If not complete, finish implementing the first production slice: PlaintextNormalizer, ManagedAesEncryptor, EncryptionResult, ResultFormatter, and any minimal namespace/project adjustments required so PlaintextNormalizerTests, ManagedAesEncryptorTests, and CliOutputModelTests pass. Run focused dotnet test commands, fix issues until those targeted tests are green, then report remaining failures (if any), especially anything left only for AesNiEncryptor.
